dt-example.title: Focus cell custom styling
dt-example.info:
When KeyTable adds focus to a table's cell it adds a class (defined by `-init keys.className` - default `-string focus`). This class can be used to provide styling information to highlight the focused cell to the end user. The stylesheets provided for KeyTable use this class and an [`outline` style](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/outline) to show the end user where the focus is in the table.
The stylesheets make use of CSS variables, allowing you to easily provide your own customisation for the styling (you can of course just write an override style for the class as well). The CSS variables used are:
* `--dtkt-focus_outline-width` * `--dtkt-focus_outline-color` * `--dtkt-focus_outline-color-selected` * `--dtkt-focus_background`
This example uses the CSS variables to set a red colour and a light red background to highlight the focused cell. Click the _CSS_ tab below the table to see the CSS used.
